incorrects.description: Student selected option B: 2 bags with 4 items each, reversed the groups and items

steps.text: Look at the first number in the expression. That tells you how many groups. How many groups do you need?

incorrects.description: Student selected option C: scattered items with no grouping structure

params.description: Option A bags highlight to show grouping structure.

steps.text: Multiplication shows equal groups. You need to see groups with items inside them. Which picture shows that?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Option A bags highlight one by one while Guide counts.

steps.text: Let me show you. The first number is groups. 4 times 2 means 4 groups. Count the bags in Option A: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four bags, four groups. The second number is in each group. Look inside one bag: 2 items. Every bag has 2. Option A shows 4 groups with 2 in each, exactly what 4 times 2 means.
